result:
RATIONALE.md is written at `jobs/brand-guide/RATIONALE.md`. I read the brief and the full 867-line guide, plus verified the assets directory, before writing it. The guide itself was not touched.

The document covers two things:

**Key decisions** (7): inlining tokens.css verbatim for portability rather than linking it; making the guide self-demonstrating (square edges, mono eyebrows, 68ch measure, reveal-once motion, zero em dashes in prose); rebuilding the capability curve as a live animated SVG instead of embedding the figma PNG exports; the canonical token picks behind the "core four" palette (notably mapping Bronze to `--amd-color-gold-reference` #CD9453 among several candidate hues); the six binding voice pairs; the CTA doctrine rendered as real buttons; and constructing the lockup specimen in CSS since no lockup asset existed.

**Open questions for Jason** (7): the most consequential are confirming the canonical bronze token, the "For Employers" CTA label (my coinage), and whether the specimen clinical values (Lp(a) 72 nmol/L, ApoB 88 mg/dL, the 163-gene panel) are approved, since people will copy them verbatim from the guide. Smaller items: one slightly stiff voice line, three now-unused curve PNGs in `assets/`, raster vs vector wordmarks, and whether the guide should eventually live next to the design system rather than in `jobs/`.

One thing I flagged explicitly in the verification section: the document contains exactly one em dash, inside the Do/Don't gallery as the intentional counterexample, encoded as an HTML entity so it doesn't trip the zero-em-dash check.
